concerns.text: Tasks T060-T063 (Behavioral Assay, Rule Space Sweep, Computational Irreducibility) have no matching FR/SC in spec.md. The spec limits scope to FR-001 through FR-007 (architecture matching, training, probing, sensitivity analysis). These tasks implement unapproved research directions (Wolfram/Kandel analogies) not authorized in the plan, creating orphaned work.

Task T052 (docs/mechanism_spec.md) and T053 (computational cost analysis) lack explicit FR/SC anchors. While T050/T051 map to FR-001 (dendritic unit implementation), T052/T053 address 'logical calculus' and 'economy' concepts not defined as requirements or success criteria in spec.md, risking scope creep.

Task T004 (config.yaml) references 'dendritic_thresholds' but the spec (FR-007) requires a sensitivity analysis sweeping specific values. The task does not explicitly mandate the values (0.1, 0.5, 0.9) defined in plan.md, creating a potential gap in FR-007 coverage if defaults are used.

Tasks T060-T063 use reviewer names (Kandel, Wolfram) as tags but reference no spec IDs. This creates stale tags referencing non-existent requirements. These tasks should be removed or mapped to a new FR if the scope is officially expanded.
